name: Aji De Carne[bolivian Peppery Pork with Banana] LF
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces boneless pork top loin, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 tablespoon aji chile powder (substitute New Mexican such as Chimayo)
1 small Havana / habanero chili pepper, optional (ground cloves, scant measure)
4 small russet potatoes, scrubbed and cut
2 green bananas, peeled and cut into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven casserole over medium heat and sauté the onions until just softened.
2. Add garlic and cook until onions are soft, about 3-4 minutes.
3. Add pork cubes and brown (char slightly) them for about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in tomatoes, saffron, salt (if needed), black pepper, chile powder, cinnamon, cloves, and broth. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 40-50 minutes.
6. Add potatoes and continue cooking until done, about 30 minutes.
7. Stir in green bananas before the potatoes are fully cooked; cook an additional 10 minutes until bananas are tender.
8. Create a well in the center of the pot and tilt to form a pool for liquids.
9. Gently combine sauce ingredients (seltzer water, milk powder, coconut extract, molasses, peanut butter).
10. Simmer until heated through, ensuring the "cream" does not boil.
11. Serve immediately with warmed tortillas and mixed salad greens, undressed or lightly dressed with lemon-lime juice.
